_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-11 13:32:08Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-11 13:32:18Z)**_

I've run the audit. Per the project's CLAUDE.md instruction, I'm **stopping here rather than running `npm audit fix`** — the dependencies are pinned and auto-bumping could break the build. Here's what `npm audit` surfaced:

## Summary: 21 vulnerabilities (1 critical, 7 high, 8 moderate, 5 low)

### ⚠️ Requires `--force` (breaking changes) — needs manual review
| Package | Severity | Issue | Fix consequence |
|---|---|---|---|
| **next** | high (many) | Cache poisoning, SSRF, XSS, DoS, request smuggling across Image Optimization / Middleware | downgrades to `next@15.5.19` (outside stated range) |
| **@ai-sdk/provider-utils** (+ `@ai-sdk/anthropic`, `@ai-sdk/react`, `@ai-sdk/ui-utils`, `ai`) | moderate | Uncontrolled resource consumption | installs `ai@6.0.201` — **major breaking change** to the core Vercel AI SDK this app is built on |
| **jsondiffpatch** | moderate | XSS via HtmlFormatter | pulled via `ai` upgrade above |
| **postcss** | moderate | XSS via unescaped `</style>` | tied to the `next` bump |

### ✅ Fixable without breaking changes (`npm audit fix`)
`@eslint/plugin-kit`, `ajv`, `brace-expansion`, `flatted` (high), `js-yaml`, `mdast-util-to-hast`, `minimatch` (high), `picomatch` (high), `rollup` (high), `tar` (high), **`vitest` (critical)**, `vite` (high), `ws` — all dev/transitive tooling deps.

## Recommendation

The most notable items:
- **`vitest` critical** (arbitrary file read/execute when UI server listens) — dev-only, but the highest severity.
- The **Next.js** cluster — these are real runtime-facing CVEs, but the only offered fix downgrades to a version outside your stated range.
- The **AI SDK** cluster — the fix is a v5→v6 major jump that would touch `provider.ts`, the chat route, tools, and `@ai-sdk/react` usage. This needs a deliberate migration, not an auto-fix.

How would you like to proceed?

- **Patch safe deps only** - I run `npm audit fix` (no --force) for the non-breaking transitive/dev deps (vitest, tar, rollup, etc.), then run the test suite. Skips next & AI SDK.
- **Plan the major upgrades** - I research and write a migration plan for the Next.js and Vercel AI SDK (v5→v6) bumps without applying anything yet.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-11 13:41:17Z)**_

Understood — stopping here with the report. No changes have been made to `package.json` or the lockfile.

**Summary of what I did:**
- Ran `npm audit` → surfaced **21 vulnerabilities (1 critical, 7 high, 8 moderate, 5 low)**.
- Did **not** run `npm audit fix` / `--force`, honoring the CLAUDE.md instruction to surface rather than auto-bump.

**The triage, for when you're ready to act:**
- **Critical:** `vitest` (dev-only — arbitrary file read/exec when the UI server listens).
- **Safe to patch** without breaking changes (`npm audit fix`): `vitest`, `vite`, `tar`, `rollup`, `picomatch`, `minimatch`, `flatted`, `brace-expansion`, `ajv`, `ws`, `js-yaml`, `mdast-util-to-hast`, `@eslint/plugin-kit` — all dev/transitive tooling.
- **Needs a deliberate migration** (`--force` only): **Next.js** (downgrades outside your stated range) and the **Vercel AI SDK cluster** (`ai` v5→v6 major — would touch `provider.ts`, the chat route, tools, and `@ai-sdk/react`).

Happy to patch the safe set, or draft a migration plan for the two major bumps, whenever you want.
